summary: Type component props with explicit interfaces for refactored hotspots
problem_statement: The refactored hotspot components (Admin, Dashboard, KanbanBoard, EditTaskDialog) lack explicitly typed prop interfaces. This makes it impossible to verify correct prop passing at compile time, increases the risk of runtime errors during the Phase 5 decomposition, and prevents IDE tooling from providing accurate completions and documentation.
in_scope: Define and export explicit TypeScript interfaces for all props of Admin, Dashboard, KanbanBoard, and EditTaskDialog components
Define prop interfaces for any child/sub-components that will be extracted during Phase 5 decomposition
Apply the new interfaces to the component signatures
Ensure npm run check passes with no new type errors after the changes
out_of_scope: Typing props for components not identified as R2 hotspots
Actual component decomposition — handled in Phase 5 (P5-R2-13 through P5-R2-16)
Changing component behaviour or adding new props
Typing server-side code — handled in P2-R2-07
requirements: Each hotspot component (Admin, Dashboard, KanbanBoard, EditTaskDialog) has an exported props interface
All component function signatures reference their props interface explicitly
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
acceptance_criteria: npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
Explicit prop interfaces exist and are exported for all four hotspot components
No runtime behaviour changes result from the typing work
dependencies: P2-R2-05 — shared/core 'any' removal should complete first so prop interfaces can reference properly typed domain entities
risks: Prop interfaces may need revision during Phase 5 decomposition as components are split; interfaces should be designed for the current structure and updated in-phase
implementation_notes: Co-locate prop interfaces with their components or in a shared types file depending on project convention. Use domain entity types (Stage, SubStage, Task) from P2-R2-05 and P2-R2-07 in prop definitions.
